name: Poha
ingredients: poha, onion, green chili, peanuts, curry leaves, mustard seeds, turmeric, lemon, salt, oil
steps: Rinse poha and drain well; Heat oil and crackle mustard seeds with curry leaves; Saute onion green chili and peanuts for 3 minutes; Add turmeric and salt then mix in poha; Finish with lemon

name: Besan Chilla
ingredients: besan, onion, tomato, green chili, coriander, turmeric, salt, water, oil
steps: Mix besan with water salt and turmeric into a smooth batter; Add onion tomato green chili and coriander; Heat a pan and spread batter; Cook both sides until golden; Serve hot

name: Curd Rice
ingredients: cooked rice, curd, milk, ginger, green chili, curry leaves, mustard seeds, salt, coriander
steps: Mash cooked rice lightly; Mix in curd milk and salt; Temper mustard seeds curry leaves ginger and green chili; Mix tempering into rice; Garnish with coriander

name: Lemon Rice
ingredients: cooked rice, lemon, peanuts, curry leaves, mustard seeds, turmeric, green chili, salt, oil
steps: Heat oil and crackle mustard seeds with curry leaves; Fry peanuts and green chili for 2 minutes; Add turmeric and salt; Toss in cooked rice; Finish with lemon juice

name: Vegetable Upma
ingredients: rava, onion, carrot, peas, green chili, curry leaves, mustard seeds, salt, oil, water
steps: Heat oil and crackle mustard seeds with curry leaves; Saute onion and vegetables for 4 minutes; Add rava and roast 2 minutes; Add water and salt and stir; Cook until fluffy

name: Masala Oats
ingredients: oats, onion, tomato, carrot, peas, green chili, turmeric, salt, oil, water
steps: Heat oil and saute onion and green chili; Add tomato and vegetables with turmeric and salt; Add oats and water; Cook until thick; Serve hot

name: Moong Dal Tadka
ingredients: yellow moong dal, onion, tomato, garlic, cumin seeds, turmeric, salt, coriander, oil
steps: Boil moong dal with turmeric and salt until soft; Heat oil and add cumin and garlic; Saute onion and tomato; Pour tadka into dal; Simmer 2 minutes and garnish

name: Palak Corn Stir Fry
ingredients: spinach, sweet corn, garlic, green chili, cumin seeds, salt, pepper, oil
steps: Heat oil and add cumin and garlic; Add corn and cook 3 minutes; Add spinach and salt; Stir until wilted; Season with pepper

name: Jeera Aloo
ingredients: potato, cumin seeds, green chili, coriander, turmeric, red chili powder, salt, oil, lemon
steps: Boil potatoes until just tender and cube; Heat oil and add cumin; Add spices and green chili; Toss potatoes and cook 6 minutes; Finish with coriander and lemon

name: Paneer Bhurji
ingredients: paneer, onion, tomato, green chili, turmeric, garam masala, salt, coriander, oil
steps: Heat oil and saute onion and green chili; Add tomato turmeric and salt; Crumble paneer and mix; Add garam masala and cook 3 minutes; Finish with coriander

name: Pan Paneer Tikka
ingredients: paneer, capsicum, onion, curd, ginger garlic paste, chili powder, garam masala, salt, oil
steps: Mix curd with spices and ginger garlic paste; Coat paneer capsicum and onion; Pan-cook on medium until charred; Turn pieces and cook evenly; Serve hot

name: Veg Pulao
ingredients: rice, mixed vegetables, onion, cumin seeds, garam masala, salt, oil, water
steps: Saute cumin and onion in oil; Add vegetables and salt; Add rice and water; Cook covered until done; Rest 5 minutes and fluff

name: Chana Chaat
ingredients: boiled chickpeas, onion, tomato, cucumber, lemon, salt, chaat masala, coriander
steps: Mix chickpeas with chopped veggies; Add salt and chaat masala; Squeeze lemon; Toss well; Serve immediately

name: Aloo Gobi Stir Fry
ingredients: potato, cauliflower, onion, turmeric, chili powder, salt, oil, coriander
steps: Heat oil and saute onion; Add potato and cauliflower with spices; Cover and cook until tender; Stir to avoid sticking; Garnish with coriander

name: Tomato Soup Indian
ingredients: tomato, garlic, black pepper, salt, butter, water, cream
steps: Saute garlic in butter; Add chopped tomato and cook soft; Add water and simmer 8 minutes; Blend if needed; Season with pepper and finish with cream

name: Veg Fried Rice
ingredients: cooked rice, onion, carrot, peas, garlic, soy sauce, black pepper, salt, oil
steps: Saute garlic and onion; Add vegetables and cook 4 minutes; Add rice soy sauce salt and pepper; Toss on high heat; Serve hot

name: Sprouts Salad
ingredients: sprouts, onion, tomato, cucumber, lemon, salt, black pepper, coriander
steps: Mix sprouts with chopped veggies; Add salt pepper and lemon; Toss well; Garnish with coriander; Serve chilled or room temp

name: Quick Veg Sandwich
ingredients: bread, butter, cucumber, tomato, onion, salt, pepper, green chutney
steps: Spread butter and chutney on bread; Layer vegetables; Sprinkle salt and pepper; Toast or serve as is; Cut and serve

name: Garlic Mushroom Stir Fry
ingredients: mushroom, garlic, onion, black pepper, salt, oil, lemon
steps: Heat oil and saute garlic and onion; Add mushrooms and salt; Stir fry on high heat until cooked; Add pepper; Finish with lemon

name: Bhindi Fry
ingredients: okra, onion, turmeric, chili powder, salt, oil, lemon
steps: Dry okra well and slice; Saute onion in oil; Add okra and spices; Cook until crisp; Finish with lemon

name: Moong Dal Khichdi
ingredients: rice, moong dal, cumin seeds, turmeric, salt, ghee, water
steps: Rinse rice and dal; Saute cumin in ghee; Add rice dal turmeric salt and water; Pressure cook or simmer until soft; Rest and serve

name: Kadhi with Pakoda
ingredients: curd, besan, onion, turmeric, salt, cumin seeds, oil, water
steps: Whisk curd with besan turmeric and salt; Simmer with water until thick; Temper cumin in oil; Add tempering and simmer 5 minutes; Serve with rice or roti

name: Quick Paneer Butter Masala
ingredients: paneer, tomato puree, butter, cream, ginger garlic paste, garam masala, salt, oil
steps: Saute ginger garlic paste in butter; Add tomato puree salt and spices; Simmer 8 minutes; Add paneer and cook 5 minutes; Finish with cream

name: Soya Chunk Curry
ingredients: soya chunks, onion, tomato, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, garam masala, salt, oil, water
steps: Soak soya chunks in hot water and squeeze; Saute onion then ginger garlic paste; Add tomato and spices; Add soya and water; Simmer until thick

name: Veg Hakka Noodles
ingredients: noodles, capsicum, carrot, cabbage, garlic, soy sauce, black pepper, salt, oil
steps: Boil noodles and drain; Saute garlic and vegetables on high heat; Add noodles soy sauce salt and pepper; Toss well; Serve hot

name: Egg Bhurji
ingredients: eggs, onion, tomato, green chili, turmeric, salt, coriander, oil
steps: Heat oil and saute onion and green chili; Add tomato turmeric and salt; Pour beaten eggs; Scramble until set; Finish with coriander

name: Anda Curry
ingredients: eggs, onion, tomato, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, salt, oil, water
steps: Boil eggs and peel; Saute onion until golden; Add ginger garlic paste tomato and spices; Add water and simmer; Add eggs and cook 5 minutes

name: Pepper Chicken Fry
ingredients: chicken, onion, garlic, black pepper, turmeric, curry leaves, salt, oil, lemon
steps: Saute onion garlic and curry leaves; Add chicken turmeric and salt; Cover and cook until done; Toss with pepper on high heat; Finish with lemon

name: Chicken Stir Fry
ingredients: chicken, capsicum, onion, garlic, green chili, soy sauce, black pepper, salt, oil
steps: Saute garlic onion and chili; Add chicken and cook through; Add capsicum soy sauce pepper; Stir fry 3 minutes; Serve hot

name: Keema Matar
ingredients: chicken mince, peas, onion, tomato, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, garam masala, salt, oil
steps: Saute onion then ginger garlic paste; Add tomato and spices; Add mince and peas; Cook until done and thick; Serve hot

name: Tuna Masala
ingredients: tuna, onion, tomato, green chili, turmeric, black pepper, salt, coriander, oil
steps: Saute onion and green chili; Add tomato turmeric and salt; Add tuna and pepper; Cook 3 minutes; Garnish with coriander

name: Prawn Fry
ingredients: prawns, garlic, green chili, turmeric, chili powder, salt, curry leaves, oil, lemon
steps: Pat dry prawns; Saute garlic chili and curry leaves; Add prawns with spices and salt; Cook until just done; Finish with lemon

name: Egg Fried Rice
ingredients: cooked rice, eggs, onion, carrot, peas, garlic, black pepper, salt, oil
steps: Scramble eggs and keep aside; Saute garlic onion and vegetables; Add rice salt and pepper; Mix eggs back; Toss and serve

name: Chicken Suji Cutlet
ingredients: chicken mince, rava, onion, green chili, ginger garlic paste, coriander, salt, oil
steps: Mix mince with rava and aromatics; Shape cutlets; Shallow fry on pan; Cook both sides until done; Serve hot

name: Quick Fish Curry
ingredients: fish, onion, tomato, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, chili powder, salt, oil, water
steps: Saute onion and ginger garlic paste; Add tomato and spices; Add water and simmer; Add fish and cook gently; Finish when fish is done

name: Pan Chicken Tikka
ingredients: chicken, curd, ginger garlic paste, chili powder, garam masala, salt, oil, lemon
steps: Marinate chicken in curd and spices; Heat pan with oil; Cook chicken pieces until charred and done; Turn and cook evenly; Finish with lemon

name: Chicken Lemon Pepper
ingredients: chicken, garlic, black pepper, salt, oil, lemon, coriander
steps: Saute garlic in oil; Add chicken and salt; Cook until done; Add pepper and toss; Finish with lemon and coriander

name: Masala Omelette
ingredients: eggs, onion, green chili, coriander, salt, black pepper, oil
steps: Beat eggs with onion chili coriander and salt; Heat pan with oil; Pour mixture and cook; Flip and cook other side; Serve hot

name: Chicken Kathi Roll
ingredients: cooked chicken, roti, onion, cucumber, lemon, salt, pepper, chutney
steps: Warm roti; Toss chicken with salt pepper and lemon; Add onion and cucumber; Roll tightly with chutney; Serve

name: Mutton Keema Quick
ingredients: mutton mince, onion, tomato, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, garam masala, salt, oil
steps: Saute onion and ginger garlic paste; Add tomato and spices; Add mince and cook until done; Reduce to desired thickness; Serve hot

name: Chicken Coconut Curry
ingredients: chicken, coconut milk, onion, tomato, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, salt, oil
steps: Saute onion and ginger garlic paste; Add tomato turmeric and salt; Add chicken and cook 10 minutes; Add coconut milk and simmer; Serve

name: Chicken Saag
ingredients: chicken, spinach, onion, garlic, turmeric, salt, oil, garam masala
steps: Saute onion and garlic; Add chicken with turmeric and salt; Add spinach and cook until wilted; Simmer until chicken is done; Finish with garam masala

name: South Indian Egg Curry
ingredients: eggs, onion, tomato, curry leaves, mustard seeds, turmeric, salt, oil, water
steps: Boil eggs and peel; Temper mustard and curry leaves; Saute onion and tomato; Add turmeric salt and water; Add eggs and simmer

name: Chicken Veg Soup
ingredients: chicken, carrot, onion, garlic, black pepper, salt, water, coriander
steps: Simmer chicken in water until cooked; Add vegetables and cook tender; Season with salt and pepper; Shred chicken and return; Garnish with coriander

name: Fish Tikka Pan
ingredients: fish, curd, ginger garlic paste, chili powder, salt, oil, lemon
steps: Marinate fish in curd and spices; Heat pan with oil; Cook fish pieces gently; Flip carefully; Finish with lemon

name: Chicken Noodles
ingredients: noodles, chicken, capsicum, onion, garlic, soy sauce, pepper, salt, oil
steps: Boil noodles and drain; Saute garlic onion and chicken; Add vegetables and cook 3 minutes; Add noodles soy sauce salt and pepper; Toss and serve

name: Basil Chicken Stir Fry
ingredients: chicken, garlic, onion, basil, black pepper, salt, oil, lemon
steps: Saute garlic and onion; Add chicken and salt; Cook until done; Add basil and pepper; Finish with lemon

name: Shakshuka Indian Style
ingredients: eggs, tomato, onion, garlic, chili powder, salt, oil, coriander
steps: Saute onion and garlic; Add tomato and spices and cook thick; Make wells and crack eggs; Cover and cook until set; Garnish with coriander

name: Tuna Salad Bowl
ingredients: tuna, lettuce, cucumber, onion, lemon, salt, black pepper, olive oil
steps: Mix tuna with lemon salt pepper and oil; Add chopped veggies; Toss well; Adjust seasoning; Serve

name: Chicken Masala Sandwich
ingredients: bread, cooked chicken, onion, tomato, butter, salt, pepper, green chutney
steps: Mix chicken with salt pepper and chutney; Spread butter on bread; Add chicken and veggies; Toast on pan; Cut and serve

name: Butter Chicken
ingredients: chicken, butter, cream, tomato puree, onion, garlic, ginger, garam masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric, kashmiri chili, kasuri methi, oil, salt
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt ginger garlic and spices for 30 min; Pan-fry chicken until cooked through; Saute onion garlic ginger in butter until golden; Add tomato puree and cook 10 min; Stir in cream garam masala and kasuri methi; Add chicken and simmer 10 min; Serve with naan or rice

name: Dal Makhani
ingredients: black lentils, kidney beans, butter, cream, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cumin, garam masala, coriander, salt
steps: Soak lentils overnight and pressure cook until soft; Saute onion garlic ginger in butter; Add tomato and cook until oil separates; Add cooked lentils and simmer 20 min; Finish with cream and garam masala; Garnish with a knob of butter

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
ingredients: chicken, yogurt, cream, tomato puree, onion, garlic, ginger, garam masala, cumin, paprika, turmeric, oil, salt
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices for 1 hour; Grill chicken until charred; Saute onion garlic ginger; Add tomato puree and spices and cook 10 min; Stir in cream; Add grilled chicken and simmer 10 min; Garnish with cream

name: Palak Paneer
ingredients: paneer, spinach, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cream, cumin, garam masala, turmeric, green chili, oil, salt
steps: Blanch spinach and blend smooth; Saute onion garlic ginger; Add tomato and cook until soft; Add spinach puree and spices; Simmer 5 min; Add paneer cubes and cream; Cook 5 min more; Serve hot

name: Chicken Biryani
ingredients: chicken, basmati rice, onion, yogurt, tomato, ginger, garlic, mint, saffron, ghee, garam masala, turmeric, oil, salt
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices; Par-cook rice with whole spices; Brown onions until crispy; Layer rice chicken and onions in a pot; Add saffron milk and ghee; Seal and cook on dum 25 min; Gently mix and serve

name: Paneer Butter Masala
ingredients: paneer, butter, cream, tomato puree, onion, cashews, garlic, ginger, garam masala, coriander, sugar, oil, salt
steps: Blend onion cashews and tomatoes; Saute in butter until thick; Add spices and cook 5 min; Stir in cream and simmer; Add paneer cubes; Cook 5 min; Finish with butter and coriander

name: Rajma Chawal
ingredients: kidney beans, rice, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, garam masala, turmeric, oil, salt
steps: Soak and pressure cook kidney beans; Cook rice separately; Saute onion garlic ginger; Add tomato and spices; Add kidney beans and simmer 15 min; Serve rajma over rice

name: Mutton Curry
ingredients: mutton, onion, tomato, yogurt, garlic, ginger, coriander, cumin, garam masala, turmeric, chili powder, oil, salt
steps: Marinate mutton in yogurt and spices; Brown onion garlic ginger; Add marinated mutton and sear; Add tomato and cook until thick; Add water and pressure cook 20 min; Simmer to thicken gravy; Garnish with coriander

name: Egg Curry
ingredients: eggs, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, oil, salt
steps: Hard boil eggs and peel; Saute onion garlic ginger in oil; Add tomato and spices; Cook until oil separates; Score eggs and add to gravy; Simmer 10 min; Garnish with coriander

name: Aloo Paratha
ingredients: whole wheat flour, potato, green chili, coriander, cumin, garam masala, butter, salt
steps: Boil and mash potatoes with spices; Make soft dough; Stuff potato mix in dough ball; Roll and cook on tawa with butter; Cook both sides golden; Serve with curd and pickle

name: Dal Baati Churma
ingredients: whole wheat flour, urad dal, chana dal, moong dal, ghee, jaggery, semolina, cardamom, cinnamon, cumin, turmeric, salt
steps: Knead hard dough and shape into balls; Bake baati in oven or over fire until golden; Pressure cook mixed dals with turmeric and salt; Temper with ghee cumin and whole spices; Crush half the baati with jaggery and ghee for churma; Serve dal and churma alongside whole baati

name: Laal Maas
ingredients: mutton, red chilies, yogurt, onion, garlic, ginger, coriander, cumin, cardamom, cloves, ghee, salt
steps: Soak dried red chilies and grind to paste; Marinate mutton in yogurt and spice paste; Brown onion in ghee; Add marinated mutton and sear well; Add remaining chili paste and cook on low heat; Pressure cook until mutton is tender; Simmer uncovered to thicken gravy

name: Gatte ki Sabzi
ingredients: besan, yogurt, turmeric, red chili, coriander, cumin, fennel, asafoetida, ghee, salt
steps: Mix besan with spices and water into a stiff dough; Roll into cylinders and boil in salted water; Slice boiled gattas; Whisk yogurt with turmeric and chili; Heat ghee temper with cumin asafoetida and fennel; Add yogurt mix and sliced gattas; Simmer until gravy thickens

name: Bajra Roti with Garlic Chutney
ingredients: bajra flour, garlic, dried red chili, salt, ghee, water
steps: Knead bajra flour with warm water and salt; Pat into thick round roti; Cook on tawa on high heat flipping often; Apply ghee; Pound garlic with dried red chili and salt for chutney; Serve hot roti with chutney and white butter

name: Pyaaz Kachori
ingredients: all-purpose flour, onion, fennel seeds, coriander, cumin, red chili, garam masala, oil, salt
steps: Make soft dough with flour oil and salt; Saute onion with fennel coriander and spices for filling; Cool the filling; Divide dough and stuff with onion filling; Seal and flatten into kachori shape; Deep fry on medium heat until golden and crisp; Serve with tamarind chutney

name: Machher Jhol
ingredients: rohu fish, potato, tomato, turmeric, cumin, green chili, ginger, mustard oil, coriander, salt
steps: Marinate fish in turmeric and salt; Fry fish in mustard oil until golden; Fry potato pieces; Saute ginger and cumin in same oil; Add tomato and cook until soft; Add fried potatoes and fish; Pour water and simmer 10 min; Finish with green chili and coriander

name: Kosha Mangsho
ingredients: mutton, onion, yogurt, ginger, garlic, turmeric, red chili, cumin, coriander, garam masala, mustard oil, salt
steps: Marinate mutton in yogurt and spices for 2 hours; Caramelize onion in mustard oil until dark brown; Add mutton and cook on high heat searing well; Lower heat and cook covered stirring often; Add small amounts of water to prevent burning; Cook until oil separates and mutton is tender; Finish with garam masala

name: Aloo Posto
ingredients: potato, poppy seeds, green chili, mustard oil, turmeric, salt, sugar
steps: Soak poppy seeds and grind with green chili to paste; Cube and lightly fry potatoes in mustard oil; Add turmeric and stir; Add poppy seed paste and mix well; Cook on low heat stirring until potatoes are coated and dry; Add a pinch of sugar and salt; Serve with rice

name: Shorshe Ilish
ingredients: hilsa fish, mustard seeds, green chili, turmeric, mustard oil, yogurt, salt
steps: Grind mustard seeds with green chili and water to a paste; Marinate hilsa in turmeric and salt; Heat mustard oil until smoking; Lightly fry fish pieces; Add mustard paste and mix; Add yogurt and green chilies; Simmer on low heat covered for 10 min; Serve with steamed rice

name: Cholar Dal
ingredients: chana dal, coconut, raisins, cardamom, bay leaf, cloves, ghee, cumin, turmeric, ginger, salt, sugar
steps: Soak and pressure cook chana dal with turmeric and salt; Heat ghee and temper with bay leaf cloves cardamom and cumin; Add ginger and cook; Add cooked dal and mix; Fry coconut pieces and raisins separately and add; Finish with a pinch of sugar; Serve with luchi or rice

name: Dhokla
ingredients: besan, yogurt, ginger, green chili, turmeric, eno, mustard seeds, curry leaves, coriander, oil, salt, sugar
steps: Mix besan yogurt ginger chili turmeric and salt into smooth batter; Rest 30 min; Add eno and mix; Pour into greased pan and steam 20 min; Cool and cut into squares; Temper mustard seeds curry leaves and green chili in oil; Pour tempering over dhokla; Garnish with coriander

name: Methi Thepla
ingredients: whole wheat flour, fenugreek leaves, yogurt, turmeric, red chili, cumin, ajwain, oil, salt
steps: Mix flour with chopped methi leaves yogurt and all spices; Knead into soft dough; Divide and roll into thin rotis; Cook on hot tawa with oil; Cook both sides until brown spots appear; Serve with yogurt and pickle; These stay fresh for 3 days and are great for travel

name: Khandvi
ingredients: besan, yogurt, ginger, turmeric, green chili, mustard seeds, sesame seeds, asafoetida, curry leaves, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Mix besan yogurt turmeric salt and water into lump-free batter; Cook on medium heat stirring continuously until thick; Quickly spread thin layer on a greased surface; Allow to cool for 2 min; Roll into tight cylinders and cut; Temper mustard seeds sesame asafoetida and curry leaves; Pour over khandvi and garnish with coriander

name: Sev Tameta
ingredients: tomato, sev, onion, garlic, ginger, green chili, turmeric, red chili, coriander, oil, salt, sugar
steps: Saute onion garlic and ginger in oil; Add tomato and cook until soft and mushy; Add turmeric red chili and salt; Add a pinch of sugar and water; Simmer 10 min until thick gravy; Top generously with sev just before serving; Garnish with coriander and serve immediately

name: Gujarati Dal
ingredients: toor dal, tomato, peanuts, jaggery, tamarind, turmeric, mustard seeds, curry leaves, cinnamon, cloves, ghee, salt
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and peanuts; Mash dal and add tamarind water; Add tomato jaggery and salt; Simmer until well combined; Heat ghee and temper with mustard seeds curry leaves cinnamon and cloves; Pour tempering into dal; Adjust sweet and sour balance; Serve with rotli and shaak

name: Vada Pav
ingredients: potato, besan, pav, garlic, green chili, ginger, mustard seeds, turmeric, dry red chili, coconut, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Boil and mash potato with tempered spices and coriander; Shape into balls; Make besan batter with turmeric and salt; Dip potato ball in batter and deep fry until golden; Make dry garlic coconut chutney by grinding roasted ingredients; Slit pav and spread green chutney; Place vada inside and top with dry garlic chutney

name: Misal Pav
ingredients: sprouted moth beans, onion, tomato, coconut, garlic, ginger, goda masala, red chili, oil, pav, coriander, lemon, farsan
steps: Pressure cook sprouts with turmeric and salt; Grind coconut garlic and goda masala for paste; Saute onion until brown add tomato and paste; Cook until oil separates; Add sprouts and water simmer 10 min; Ladle into bowls top with raw onion coriander and farsan; Squeeze lemon and serve with pav

name: Pav Bhaji
ingredients: potato, cauliflower, peas, capsicum, tomato, onion, garlic, ginger, pav bhaji masala, butter, pav, lemon, coriander
steps: Boil potato cauliflower and peas and mash; Saute onion garlic ginger in butter; Add capsicum and tomato and cook well; Add mashed vegetables and pav bhaji masala; Mash everything together on tawa adding butter; Cook on high heat stirring and mashing; Toast pav with butter; Garnish with onion lemon and coriander

name: Puran Poli
ingredients: chana dal, jaggery, cardamom, nutmeg, all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, turmeric, ghee, oil
steps: Pressure cook chana dal until soft and drain completely; Mash with jaggery cardamom and nutmeg for puran; Knead dough with flour turmeric and oil; Stuff dough ball with puran and roll gently; Cook on tawa on medium heat; Apply ghee and cook until golden; Serve with warm ghee and milk

name: Kolhapuri Chicken
ingredients: chicken, onion, coconut, sesame seeds, poppy seeds, red chili, coriander, cumin, garam masala, oil, salt
steps: Dry roast coconut sesame poppy seeds and whole spices; Grind into fine powder; Caramelize onion and grind; Sear chicken pieces in oil; Add onion paste and cook; Add ground spice powder and mix well; Add water and simmer covered 25 min; Finish with garam masala; Serve with bhakri or roti

name: Sabudana Khichdi
ingredients: sabudana, potato, peanuts, green chili, ginger, cumin, ghee, lemon, coriander, salt, sugar
steps: Soak sabudana 4 hours and drain completely; Roast and crush peanuts; Cube and fry potato until golden; Heat ghee and temper with cumin; Add green chili and ginger; Add potato and sabudana; Toss gently with salt and sugar; Cook covered on low heat 8 min; Finish with lemon and coriander

name: Goan Fish Curry
ingredients: pomfret, coconut, dried red chili, coriander seeds, cumin, turmeric, tamarind, garlic, ginger, onion, oil, salt
steps: Grind coconut red chili coriander cumin and turmeric with water to paste; Saute onion garlic ginger in oil; Add ground paste and cook 5 min; Add tamarind water and bring to boil; Add fish pieces carefully; Simmer gently 10 min until fish is cooked; Do not stir too much; Serve with steamed rice

name: Prawn Balchao
ingredients: prawns, onion, garlic, ginger, red chili, vinegar, tomato, mustard seeds, fenugreek, oil, salt, sugar
steps: Grind red chili garlic ginger with vinegar to paste; Saute onion in oil until dark; Add balchao paste and cook until oil separates; Add tomato and cook well; Add cleaned prawns and stir; Add salt and a little sugar; Cook until prawns are done and gravy is thick; Serve with bread or rice

name: Chicken Xacuti
ingredients: chicken, coconut, poppy seeds, dried red chili, coriander, cumin, cloves, cinnamon, nutmeg, onion, garlic, tamarind, oil, salt
steps: Dry roast coconut poppy seeds and whole spices; Grind with red chili and water to paste; Sear chicken in oil until browned; Saute onion and garlic until golden; Add ground masala and cook 10 min; Add tamarind water and chicken; Simmer covered 25 min until chicken is tender; Rest 10 min before serving

name: Kerala Fish Curry
ingredients: king fish, coconut milk, kokum, shallots, green chili, ginger, turmeric, red chili, curry leaves, coconut oil, salt
steps: Heat coconut oil and saute shallots ginger green chili and curry leaves; Add turmeric and red chili powder; Add thin coconut milk and kokum; Bring to simmer and add fish pieces; Cook gently 10 min; Add thick coconut milk and reduce heat; Simmer 5 min without boiling; Serve with red rice

name: Puttu with Kadala Curry
ingredients: rice flour, coconut, black chickpeas, onion, garlic, ginger, turmeric, red chili, coriander, coconut milk, curry leaves, coconut oil, salt
steps: Mix rice flour with salt and water until it resembles wet sand; Layer rice flour and grated coconut in puttu maker; Steam until cooked through; Pressure cook black chickpeas; Saute onion garlic ginger and spices in coconut oil; Add chickpeas and coconut milk; Simmer until thick; Serve puttu with curry

name: Avial
ingredients: drumstick, raw banana, yam, carrot, beans, coconut, green chili, cumin, yogurt, curry leaves, coconut oil, turmeric, salt
steps: Cut all vegetables lengthwise and cook in minimal water with turmeric and salt; Grind coconut cumin and green chili to coarse paste; Add coconut paste to half-cooked vegetables; Mix in yogurt and cook on low heat; Finish with fresh curry leaves and coconut oil; Serve with rice or as part of sadya

name: Kerala Prawn Moilee
ingredients: prawns, coconut milk, onion, green chili, ginger, garlic, turmeric, mustard seeds, curry leaves, coconut oil, salt
steps: Saute onion ginger garlic in coconut oil until soft; Add green chili and turmeric; Add thin coconut milk and bring to simmer; Add prawns and cook 5 min; Add thick coconut milk and curry leaves; Simmer gently without boiling 3 min; Adjust salt; Serve with appam or rice

name: Rogan Josh
ingredients: lamb, onion, yogurt, kashmiri red chili, fennel, ginger powder, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf, ghee, salt
steps: Heat ghee and add whole spices; Brown lamb pieces well; Add kashmiri red chili paste for deep red color; Add beaten yogurt gradually stirring continuously; Add ginger powder and fennel powder; Cook on low heat covered until lamb is tender; Uncover and reduce gravy; Finish with garam masala

name: Kashmiri Dum Aloo
ingredients: small potato, yogurt, fennel, ginger powder, kashmiri red chili, cardamom, cloves, asafoetida, ghee, salt
steps: Boil and prick potatoes all over; Fry in ghee until golden; Remove and lightly flatten; In same ghee add asafoetida and spices; Add kashmiri chili paste; Add beaten yogurt stirring constantly; Add fennel and ginger powder; Add potatoes and cook on dum; Simmer 20 min until oil floats

name: Yakhni Pulao
ingredients: basmati rice, lamb, yogurt, onion, fennel seeds, cardamom, cloves, bay leaf, cinnamon, ginger, garlic, ghee, saffron, salt
steps: Cook lamb with onion whole spices and water to make yakhni stock; Remove lamb and strain stock; Soak rice and cook in yakhni until 70 percent done; Layer fried onion and lamb pieces; Drizzle saffron milk; Seal and cook on dum 20 min; Rest 10 min before serving

name: Hyderabadi Biryani
ingredients: basmati rice, chicken, yogurt, onion, mint, coriander, saffron, ghee, rose water, kewra, garam masala, biryani masala, oil, salt
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt and biryani masala 2 hours; Fry onion until golden and crispy; Parboil rice with whole spices until 70 percent done; Layer raw marinated chicken in heavy pot; Add fried onion mint and coriander; Layer rice on top; Drizzle saffron milk ghee and rose water; Seal with dough and cook on dum 45 min

name: Mirchi ka Salan
ingredients: long green chili, peanuts, sesame seeds, coconut, tamarind, onion, cumin, mustard seeds, curry leaves, turmeric, oil, salt
steps: Dry roast peanuts sesame and coconut; Grind with tamarind and water; Slit and fry green chilies until skin changes; Temper mustard cumin and curry leaves; Add onion and fry until brown; Add ground paste and cook until oil separates; Add tamarind water and fried chilies; Simmer 15 min; Serve with biryani

name: Chettinad Chicken Curry
ingredients: chicken, onion, tomato, coconut, black pepper, fennel, kalpasi, marathi mokku, dried red chili, curry leaves, ginger, garlic, oil, salt
steps: Dry roast whole spices including kalpasi and marathi mokku; Grind with coconut to paste; Saute onion until golden; Add ginger garlic paste and cook; Add tomato and cook until soft; Add chicken and sear; Add ground masala and curry leaves; Add water and simmer covered 30 min; Reduce until thick

name: Pani Puri
ingredients: semolina, all-purpose flour, mint, coriander, green chili, ginger, tamarind, cumin, black salt, potato, chickpeas, chaat masala, water
steps: Knead semolina dough rest 30 min; Roll thin and cut circles; Deep fry on medium heat pressing to puff; Blend mint coriander green chili and cumin with tamarind and cold water; Strain and add black salt; Mash boiled potato with chickpeas and chaat masala; Poke puri fill with potato and pour pani; Eat immediately

name: Bhel Puri
ingredients: puffed rice, sev, puri, potato, onion, tomato, tamarind chutney, green chutney, coriander, lemon, chaat masala, salt
steps: Mix puffed rice with boiled potato onion and tomato; Add tamarind chutney and green chutney; Toss with chaat masala and salt; Add broken puris and sev; Squeeze lemon juice; Toss quickly and serve immediately; Add more chutneys to taste; Best eaten within 2 minutes

name: Aloo Tikki Chaat
ingredients: potato, bread, cornflour, green pea, cumin, coriander, ginger, green chili, yogurt, tamarind chutney, green chutney, sev, onion, chaat masala, oil
steps: Mash potato with spices peas and cornflour; Shape into flat tikkis; Shallow fry until crispy on both sides; Place tikkis on plate; Top with whisked yogurt; Drizzle tamarind and green chutney; Sprinkle sev and chopped onion; Finish with chaat masala and coriander

name: Dahi Vada
ingredients: urad dal, yogurt, ginger, green chili, cumin, tamarind chutney, green chutney, coriander, red chili, oil, salt, sugar
steps: Soak and grind urad dal to fluffy batter; Add ginger and salt; Deep fry spoonfuls until golden; Soak fried vadas in warm water 10 min and squeeze; Whisk yogurt with sugar and salt; Arrange vadas in bowl and cover with yogurt; Drizzle tamarind and green chutney; Garnish with cumin powder red chili and coriander

name: Idli Sambar
ingredients: idli rice, urad dal, toor dal, drumstick, tomato, onion, tamarind, sambar powder, mustard seeds, curry leaves, asafoetida, oil, salt
steps: Soak and grind idli rice and urad dal; Ferment batter overnight; Steam in idli moulds 12 min; Pressure cook toor dal with vegetables and tamarind; Add sambar powder and boil; Temper mustard seeds curry leaves and asafoetida; Add to sambar and simmer; Serve hot idli with sambar and coconut chutney

name: Masala Dosa
ingredients: idli rice, urad dal, potato, onion, green chili, ginger, mustard seeds, curry leaves, turmeric, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Use fermented dosa batter; Boil and mash potato with tempered onion and spices for filling; Heat iron tawa until very hot; Pour batter and spread thinly in circles; Drizzle oil on edges; Place potato filling on one side; Fold and cook until crispy golden; Serve with sambar and coconut chutney

name: Uttapam
ingredients: idli rice, urad dal, onion, tomato, green chili, coriander, carrot, oil, salt
steps: Pour thick dosa batter on medium hot tawa; Do not spread too thin; Arrange chopped onion tomato chili coriander on top; Press gently into batter; Drizzle oil around edges; Cover and cook on low heat until set; Flip and cook other side until golden; Serve with chutney and sambar

name: Pesarattu
ingredients: green moong dal, rice, ginger, green chili, onion, cumin, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Soak green moong overnight; Grind with rice ginger and green chili to smooth batter; Add salt and water for consistency; Spread thin on hot tawa; Sprinkle finely chopped onion cumin and coriander; Cook on medium heat; Drizzle oil and fold when edges crisp; Serve with ginger chutney and upma

name: Medu Vada
ingredients: urad dal, ginger, green chili, curry leaves, coriander, black pepper, oil, salt
steps: Soak and grind urad dal to thick fluffy batter; Add ginger green chili curry leaves pepper and salt; Mix well and whip with hands for fluffiness; Wet hands and shape into ring with hole; Deep fry in hot oil until golden; Drain on paper; Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar

name: Gulab Jamun
ingredients: milk powder, all-purpose flour, khoya, baking soda, ghee, sugar, cardamom, rose water, saffron
steps: Make sugar syrup with cardamom rose water and saffron; Mix milk powder flour khoya and baking soda; Add ghee and enough milk to make soft dough; Shape into smooth balls without cracks; Deep fry on very low heat until dark golden brown; Soak in warm syrup for 30 min; Serve warm or at room temperature

name: Gajar Halwa
ingredients: carrot, milk, sugar, khoya, ghee, cardamom, cashews, raisins, almonds
steps: Grate carrots and cook in milk on medium heat stirring often; Cook until all milk is absorbed; Add sugar and stir until dissolved; Add ghee and khoya; Cook until mixture leaves sides of pan; Add cardamom; Fry cashews and raisins in ghee and add; Serve hot or warm

name: Rice Kheer
ingredients: basmati rice, full cream milk, sugar, cardamom, saffron, cashews, raisins, almonds, rose water
steps: Wash and soak rice 20 min; Boil milk and add drained rice; Simmer on low heat stirring frequently; Cook until rice is soft and milk thickens; Add sugar and cardamom; Add saffron soaked in warm milk; Add rose water and nuts; Serve warm or chilled

name: Rasgulla
ingredients: full cream milk, lemon juice, sugar, cardamom, rose water
steps: Curdle milk with lemon juice and drain through muslin; Hang chenna 30 min to remove excess water; Knead chenna until smooth and non-sticky; Shape into smooth balls; Boil sugar syrup with cardamom; Drop balls gently and cover; Cook on medium heat 15 min until spongy; Cool and soak in syrup with rose water

name: Besan Ladoo
ingredients: besan, ghee, powdered sugar, cardamom, cashews, raisins
steps: Roast besan in ghee on low heat stirring continuously for 20 min until fragrant and golden; Cool slightly; Add powdered sugar and cardamom and mix well; Add lightly fried cashews and raisins; When mixture is warm enough to handle shape into round ladoos; Allow to set at room temperature; Store in airtight container

name: Kadai Chicken
ingredients: chicken, capsicum, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, coriander seeds, dried red chili, kasuri methi, oil, garam masala, salt
steps: Coarsely grind coriander seeds and dried red chili for kadai masala; Saute onion garlic ginger in oil; Add tomato and cook until thick; Add chicken and kadai masala; Cook on high heat; Add capsicum pieces and toss; Simmer covered 15 min until chicken is done; Finish with kasuri methi and garam masala

name: Malai Kofta
ingredients: paneer, potato, cashews, cream, onion, tomato, cardamom, cloves, kasuri methi, ghee, salt, sugar
steps: Grate paneer mix with mashed potato cashews and spices; Shape into balls and deep fry; Boil onion tomato and cashews and blend smooth; Heat ghee and cook paste until oil separates; Add cream and water; Add cardamom and kasuri methi; Simmer 10 min; Place koftas in gravy just before serving

name: Sarson ka Saag
ingredients: mustard leaves, spinach, bathua, makki flour, garlic, ginger, green chili, ghee, onion, tomato, salt
steps: Wash and chop mustard spinach and bathua leaves; Pressure cook all leaves with green chili; Cool and blend roughly; Cook ground greens with makki flour on low heat stirring often for 30 min; Temper chopped garlic ginger onion in ghee until golden; Add tomato and cook; Mix into saag; Serve with makki roti and white butter

name: Chole Bhature
ingredients: kabuli chana, all-purpose flour, yogurt, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, chole masala, pomegranate powder, bay leaf, oil, baking soda, salt
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight and pressure cook with tea bag and spices; Knead dough with flour yogurt baking soda and oil; Rest dough 2 hours; Prepare chole gravy with onion tomato masala and pomegranate powder; Roll bhature thick and deep fry until puffed golden; Serve piping hot chole and bhature with onion and pickle

name: Baingan Bharta
ingredients: eggplant, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, green chili, cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, oil, coriander leaves, salt
steps: Roast eggplant directly on flame until charred and soft; Peel and mash well; Heat oil and add cumin; Saute garlic ginger and green chili; Add onion and cook until golden; Add tomato and cook until mushy; Add mashed eggplant and spices; Cook on high heat stirring; Finish with garam masala and coriander

name: Tomato Rasam
ingredients: tomato, toor dal, tamarind, cumin, pepper, mustard seeds, dried red chili, curry leaves, asafoetida, garlic, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Cook toor dal and extract liquid; Boil tomato tamarind and rasam powder; Add dal water and simmer; Temper mustard seeds dried red chili curry leaves asafoetida and garlic in oil; Pour into rasam and boil briefly; Finish with coriander; Serve as soup or pour over rice with ghee

name: Sambar
ingredients: toor dal, drumstick, carrot, shallots, tomato, tamarind, sambar powder, mustard seeds, curry leaves, asafoetida, oil, salt
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric; Cook vegetables separately with tamarind water; Add cooked dal to vegetables; Add sambar powder and boil; Temper mustard seeds curry leaves shallots and asafoetida; Add to sambar and simmer 10 min; Adjust consistency; Serve with idli dosa or rice

name: Masoor Dal Tadka
ingredients: masoor dal, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cumin, turmeric, red chili, garam masala, ghee, coriander, salt
steps: Pressure cook masoor dal with turmeric until soft; Heat ghee and temper cumin until it splutters; Saute garlic ginger and onion until golden; Add tomato and cook until oil separates; Add red chili and garam masala; Add cooked dal and water; Simmer 10 min; Finish with coriander; Serve with rice or roti

name: Chana Dal Tadka
ingredients: chana dal, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, green chili, cumin, turmeric, coriander, kasuri methi, ghee, salt
steps: Soak and pressure cook chana dal with turmeric; Heat ghee and temper cumin; Saute garlic ginger and green chili; Add onion until golden; Add tomato and cook through; Add cooked dal and spices; Simmer until desired consistency; Add kasuri methi and coriander; Serve with rice or chapati

name: Rajma Chawal
ingredients: kidney beans, basmati rice, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, garam masala, turmeric, red chili, oil, salt
steps: Soak kidney beans overnight and pressure cook until soft; Saute onion garlic ginger until golden; Add tomato and spices and cook until oil separates; Add cooked beans and mix well; Simmer 15 min adding water as needed; Cook basmati rice separately; Serve rajma over rice with onion salad

name: Palak Paneer
ingredients: spinach, paneer, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cream, cumin, garam masala, kasuri methi, oil, salt
steps: Blanch spinach in boiling water and blend smooth; Saute onion garlic ginger in oil; Add tomato and cook until oil separates; Add blended spinach and mix; Add cubed paneer and cream; Simmer 10 min; Add kasuri methi and garam masala; Serve with roti or naan

name: Matar Paneer
ingredients: paneer, green peas, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, garam masala, kasuri methi, oil, salt
steps: Saute onion garlic ginger until golden; Add tomato and spices and cook until thick; Add blanched peas and fried paneer cubes; Add water and simmer 10 min; Finish with kasuri methi and garam masala; Serve with roti or rice

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
ingredients: chicken, yogurt, cream, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, tikka masala, kasuri methi, butter, oil, salt
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt and tikka masala 2 hours; Grill or cook in oven until charred; Saute onion garlic ginger in butter; Add tomato and cook until thick; Add grilled chicken and cream; Simmer 10 min; Finish with kasuri methi; Serve with naan or rice

name: Rava Upma
ingredients: semolina, onion, green chili, ginger, carrot, peas, mustard seeds, curry leaves, cashews, ghee, salt
steps: Dry roast semolina until fragrant and light golden; Heat ghee and fry cashews; Temper mustard seeds curry leaves and green chili; Saute onion ginger and vegetables; Add boiling water and salt; Add roasted semolina stirring constantly; Cook covered on low heat 5 min; Fluff and serve hot

name: Vegetable Biryani
ingredients: basmati rice, mixed vegetables, yogurt, onion, mint, saffron, ghee, whole spices, biryani masala, rose water, salt
steps: Parboil rice with whole spices until 60 percent done; Marinate vegetables in yogurt and biryani masala; Cook vegetables until half done; Layer vegetables in heavy pot; Add fried onion and mint; Layer rice on top; Drizzle saffron milk ghee and rose water; Seal and cook on dum 25 min; Rest before serving

name: Lassi
ingredients: yogurt, sugar, cardamom, rose water, milk, ice, saffron, pistachio
steps: Whisk thick yogurt smooth; Add sugar cardamom and rose water; Add cold milk and ice; Blend until frothy; Pour in tall glasses; Garnish with saffron strands and crushed pistachio; Serve immediately

name: Egg Fried Rice
ingredients: cooked rice, egg, carrot, beans, capsicum, onion, garlic, soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, sesame oil, oil, salt
steps: Use day-old cold rice for best results; Scramble eggs in hot wok and remove; Stir fry garlic vegetables on high heat; Add rice and toss on high flame; Add soy sauce vinegar and pepper; Add scrambled eggs back; Drizzle sesame oil and toss; Serve immediately

name: Chicken Manchurian
ingredients: chicken, cornflour, all-purpose flour, egg, garlic, ginger, green chili, soy sauce, vinegar, tomato ketchup, capsicum, spring onion, oil, salt, pepper
steps: Mix chicken with cornflour flour egg and spices; Deep fry until golden and crispy; Make sauce by stir frying garlic ginger chili in very hot oil; Add soy sauce ketchup vinegar and water; Thicken with cornflour slurry; Toss fried chicken in sauce; Add capsicum and spring onion; Serve immediately

name: Veg Hakka Noodles
ingredients: hakka noodles, carrot, cabbage, capsicum, onion, spring onion, garlic, soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, sesame oil, oil, salt
steps: Boil noodles until just done drain and toss with oil; Heat wok until smoking; Stir fry garlic on high heat; Add vegetables and toss on high flame; Add noodles and toss vigorously; Add soy sauce vinegar and pepper; Drizzle sesame oil; Garnish with spring onion; Serve hot

name: Paneer Chilli
ingredients: paneer, capsicum, onion, garlic, ginger, green chili, soy sauce, tomato ketchup, vinegar, cornflour, spring onion, sesame oil, oil, salt, pepper
steps: Cut paneer into cubes coat with cornflour and fry until golden; Make sauce with soy sauce ketchup vinegar and cornflour slurry; Stir fry garlic ginger and green chili in hot oil; Add onion and capsicum and toss; Add sauce and bring to boil; Add fried paneer and toss; Drizzle sesame oil; Garnish with spring onion

name: Gond ke Ladoo
ingredients: edible gum, whole wheat flour, ghee, jaggery, dried ginger, cardamom, nutmeg, almonds, cashews, poppy seeds
steps: Fry edible gum in ghee until it puffs up; Remove and crush roughly; Roast wheat flour in same ghee until golden and fragrant; Melt jaggery with a little water; Mix flour gond nuts and spices; Add jaggery syrup and bind into ladoos while warm; Store in airtight jar; Best eaten in winter for warmth

name: Til ke Ladoo
ingredients: sesame seeds, jaggery, peanuts, desiccated coconut, cardamom, ghee
steps: Dry roast sesame seeds until golden and fragrant; Roast peanuts and remove skins roughly; Melt jaggery in a pan until one-thread consistency; Add sesame coconut and peanuts quickly; Add cardamom and mix fast; Shape into round balls while mixture is still warm; Work quickly before it hardens

name: Methi Matar Malai
ingredients: fenugreek leaves, green peas, cream, onion, cashews, ginger, garlic, cardamom, mace, white pepper, ghee, salt
steps: Blanch methi leaves in salted water and squeeze dry; Boil onion cashews and ginger and blend to white gravy; Heat ghee and cook white gravy with cardamom and mace; Add methi and peas and stir; Add cream and white pepper; Simmer gently without boiling; Serve with naan or paratha

name: Mooli Paratha
ingredients: whole wheat flour, white radish, green chili, ginger, ajwain, coriander, ghee, salt
steps: Grate radish squeeze out all water; Mix with green chili ginger ajwain coriander and salt; Make soft dough with flour; Stuff radish filling and roll into paratha; Cook on tawa on medium heat with ghee; Cook both sides until golden and crispy; Serve with white butter and curd

name: Shalgam Gosht
ingredients: mutton, turnip, onion, yogurt, garlic, ginger, turmeric, red chili, coriander, cumin, garam masala, ghee, salt
steps: Brown onion in ghee until deep golden; Add garlic ginger and spices; Add mutton and sear on high heat; Add yogurt and cook until oil separates; Add turnip cubes and mix; Add water and pressure cook 20 min; Simmer uncovered to thicken; Finish with garam masala; Serve with naan

name: Gajar Matar Sabzi
ingredients: carrot, green peas, onion, tomato, ginger, cumin, turmeric, coriander, garam masala, oil, salt, coriander leaves
steps: Heat oil and temper cumin; Add onion and ginger and saute; Add tomato and spices and cook until thick; Add diced carrot and peas; Mix well and add splash of water; Cover and cook on low heat 10 min until carrots are tender; Finish with garam masala and coriander; Serve with roti

name: Panjiri
ingredients: whole wheat flour, ghee, sugar, almonds, cashews, raisins, dried coconut, makhana, dried ginger, cardamom, edible gum
steps: Roast flour in ghee on low heat stirring continuously until fragrant golden; Cool completely; Fry edible gum in ghee until puffed; Fry makhana until crispy; Mix all ingredients with sugar while still warm; Adjust sweetness; Store in airtight container; Traditionally given to new mothers in winter

name: Aam Panna
ingredients: raw mango, sugar, black salt, roasted cumin, mint, black pepper, water, ice
steps: Pressure cook or boil raw mango until soft; Cool and scoop out pulp; Blend pulp with sugar black salt roasted cumin and black pepper; Strain concentrate; Refrigerate concentrate; To serve dilute with cold water and ice; Add fresh mint; Adjust salt and sweetness; Prevents heatstroke

name: Thandai
ingredients: milk, almonds, cashews, poppy seeds, fennel seeds, melon seeds, black pepper, rose petals, cardamom, saffron, sugar
steps: Soak almonds cashews poppy seeds melon seeds and fennel overnight; Grind soaked ingredients with rose petals and black pepper to fine paste; Boil milk with saffron and cardamom; Cool completely; Blend milk with ground paste and strain; Add sugar and stir until dissolved; Chill and serve over ice; Traditional Holi drink

name: Sattu Sharbat
ingredients: roasted gram flour, water, lemon, black salt, roasted cumin, mint, ginger, sugar or jaggery, ice
steps: Add sattu to a glass; Mix with cold water first to avoid lumps; Add lemon juice black salt roasted cumin and grated ginger; Add jaggery or sugar to taste; Stir vigorously until sattu is completely dissolved; Add ice and fresh mint; Serve immediately; Bihar summer staple that cools the body

name: Sol Kadhi
ingredients: kokum, coconut milk, garlic, green chili, coriander, cumin, salt, water
steps: Soak kokum in warm water 10 min and extract juice; Grind garlic green chili cumin and coriander; Add ground paste to kokum water; Stir in fresh coconut milk; Add salt and mix; Strain through sieve for smooth texture; Chill thoroughly; Serve as digestive drink or side with Goan fish dishes; Naturally cooling

name: Raw Mango Dal
ingredients: toor dal, raw mango, tomato, green chili, turmeric, mustard seeds, cumin, curry leaves, asafoetida, dried red chili, oil, salt, jaggery
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and diced raw mango; Mash well; Add jaggery and salt for sweet sour balance; Temper mustard seeds cumin dried red chili curry leaves and asafoetida in oil; Add chopped tomato and green chili; Cook 3 min; Pour into dal and simmer; Serve with rice and papad

name: Pudina Chaas
ingredients: yogurt, mint, green chili, ginger, roasted cumin, black salt, water, ice
steps: Blend mint leaves green chili ginger and a little water into paste; Whisk yogurt smooth; Add water to thin it; Add mint paste through a strainer; Season with black salt and roasted cumin; Adjust consistency with cold water; Add ice and serve immediately; Perfect summer coolant after a meal

name: Mixed Vegetable Pakora
ingredients: onion, potato, spinach, green chili, besan, rice flour, ajwain, turmeric, red chili, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Slice onion thinly and potato in thin rounds; Make thick batter with besan rice flour turmeric ajwain chili and salt; Add sliced vegetables and mix to coat; Ensure batter is thick enough to cling; Heat oil on medium; Drop spoonfuls and fry until golden crispy; Serve with mint chutney and chai; Best on rainy evenings

name: Bhutta Masala
ingredients: corn on cob, butter, lemon, red chili powder, chaat masala, black salt, cumin
steps: Roast corn directly over open flame turning often until charred and cooked; Alternatively grill on high heat; Rub butter generously over hot corn; Mix red chili chaat masala cumin and black salt; Rub spice mix over buttered corn; Squeeze lemon and rub the squeezed lemon over corn; Serve immediately; Classic monsoon street food

name: Kanda Bhaji
ingredients: onion, besan, green chili, ginger, turmeric, red chili, ajwain, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Slice onion very thinly and separate rings; Add besan green chili ginger ajwain and spices; Mix without adding water; Onion releases enough moisture to bind batter; Rest 10 min; Deep fry small portions in hot oil until crispy golden; Drain well on paper; Serve with green chutney; Maharashtrian monsoon staple

name: Mirchi Bajji
ingredients: large green chili, besan, rice flour, turmeric, ajwain, cumin, oil, salt, potato stuffing, tamarind, coriander
steps: Make thick batter with besan rice flour and spices; Slit large chilies and remove seeds to reduce heat; Stuff with mashed spiced potato and tamarind; Dip in batter and deep fry until golden; Drain on paper; Serve immediately with coconut chutney; Popular Andhra street snack perfect for monsoon

name: Masala Corn
ingredients: sweet corn, butter, onion, tomato, green chili, lemon, chaat masala, coriander, black salt
steps: Boil or microwave sweet corn kernels; Heat butter in pan; Add finely chopped onion and chili; Saute 2 min; Add tomato and toss; Add corn and mix; Season with chaat masala black salt and lemon; Toss on high heat; Garnish with coriander; Serve in paper cups like street style

name: Modak
ingredients: rice flour, coconut, jaggery, cardamom, ghee, salt, water, poppy seeds
steps: Cook rice flour with boiling salted water to make soft dough; Add ghee and knead smooth; Make filling by cooking coconut jaggery cardamom and poppy seeds together; Cool filling; Shape dough into small cups; Fill with coconut mixture and pinch to form modak shape with pleats; Steam 15 min; Serve with ghee; Offered to Ganesha

name: Gujiya
ingredients: all-purpose flour, khoya, sugar, almonds, raisins, cardamom, desiccated coconut, ghee, oil
steps: Make stiff dough with flour ghee and water; Mix khoya with sugar cardamom coconut almonds and raisins for filling; Roll small circles; Place filling on one half; Fold and crimp edges into half moon shape; Deep fry on medium heat until golden; Cool completely; Store in airtight box; Traditional Holi sweet

name: Chakli
ingredients: rice flour, urad dal flour, besan, cumin, sesame seeds, ajwain, asafoetida, butter, turmeric, red chili, salt
steps: Mix all flours with spices butter and enough water to make firm dough; Load into chakli press with star attachment; Press directly into hot oil in circular motion; Fry on medium heat until crispy and golden; Drain on paper; Cool completely before storing; Should be crispy for at least a week in airtight container; Diwali essential

name: Mathri
ingredients: all-purpose flour, semolina, ajwain, cumin, black pepper, kasuri methi, ghee, oil, salt
steps: Mix flour semolina spices and ghee until crumbly; Add water gradually to make stiff dough; Roll thick and cut into discs or squares; Prick with fork to prevent puffing; Deep fry on low medium heat until golden and cooked through; Takes 8-10 min per batch; Drain and cool completely; Serve with pickle and chai

name: Karanji
ingredients: all-purpose flour, desiccated coconut, jaggery, sesame seeds, poppy seeds, cardamom, nutmeg, ghee, oil
steps: Knead stiff dough with flour ghee and water; Prepare filling with roasted coconut jaggery sesame and poppy seeds; Add cardamom and nutmeg; Roll thin circles; Place filling and fold into half moon; Press edges with fork or make rope pattern; Deep fry on medium heat until golden; Traditional Diwali and Goa celebration sweet

name: Dalma
ingredients: toor dal, raw papaya, potato, raw banana, beans, tomato, turmeric, cumin, dried red chili, ginger, ghee, panch phoran, salt
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric; Boil all vegetables separately in salted water until just done; Add cooked vegetables to dal and simmer; Heat ghee and crackle panch phoran with dried red chili and ginger; Pour tempering into dal; Simmer 5 min; Serve with pakhala or rice; Odisha daily staple and temple prasad

name: Pakhala Bhata
ingredients: cooked rice, water, yogurt, cucumber, onion, green chili, coriander, roasted cumin, mustard seeds, curry leaves, salt
steps: Soak cooked rice in water overnight to ferment slightly; In morning mix with yogurt and salt; Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves and add; Serve with raw cucumber onion green chili and coriander alongside; Garnish with roasted cumin; Eat cold; Traditional summer cooling meal of Odisha; Pairs with dalma and fried vegetables

name: Chenna Poda
ingredients: fresh paneer, sugar, semolina, cardamom, cashews, raisins, ghee
steps: Knead fresh paneer until smooth; Mix in sugar semolina and cardamom; Caramelize sugar in baking dish with ghee; Pour paneer mixture over caramel; Top with cashews and raisins; Bake in oven at 200C for 45 min until dark golden; Cool completely and flip onto plate; Caramel coats the top; Odisha unique baked cheese dessert

name: Macha Besara
ingredients: rohu fish, mustard seeds, garlic, green chili, tomato, turmeric, panch phoran, mustard oil, salt, coriander
steps: Grind mustard seeds garlic and green chili with water to paste; Marinate fish with turmeric and salt; Fry fish in mustard oil and remove; Crackle panch phoran in same oil; Add mustard paste and cook 3 min; Add tomato and cook until soft; Add fried fish and water; Simmer 8 min; Serve with rice

name: Masor Tenga
ingredients: rohu fish, tomato, lemon, turmeric, mustard oil, fenugreek seeds, green chili, coriander, salt
steps: Marinate fish in turmeric and salt; Fry fish in mustard oil until golden and remove; Crackle fenugreek seeds in same oil; Add chopped tomato and green chili; Cook until tomato softens; Add fried fish and water; Simmer 10 min; Add lemon juice generously; Garnish with coriander; Light sour fish curry of Assam; Eaten with rice

name: Aloo Pitika
ingredients: potato, mustard oil, onion, green chili, coriander, salt, mustard seeds
steps: Boil potatoes until soft and peel; Mash roughly; Add raw mustard oil generously; Mix in finely chopped onion and green chili; Add salt and coriander; Crush mustard seeds and add; Mix well with hands; Adjust oil and salt; Serve immediately as a side; Assamese comfort food eaten with rice and masor tenga

name: Duck Curry Assamese
ingredients: duck, potato, onion, garlic, ginger, turmeric, cumin, coriander, mustard oil, black pepper, bay leaf, salt
steps: Clean duck and cut into pieces; Marinate with turmeric and salt; Heat mustard oil and brown onion; Add garlic ginger and whole spices; Add duck and sear well on high heat; Duck releases its own fat; Add potato and spices; Add water and pressure cook 30 min; Simmer uncovered until gravy thickens; Festive Assamese dish

name: Gongura Mutton
ingredients: mutton, gongura leaves, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, green chili, cumin, coriander, turmeric, oil, salt
steps: Pressure cook gongura leaves with green chili and garlic; Grind to paste; Brown onion garlic ginger in oil; Add mutton and sear well; Add spices and tomato; Cook until oil separates; Add gongura paste and mix; Add water and pressure cook 20 min; Simmer to reduce gravy; The sorrel leaves give distinctive sour flavor

name: Andhra Chicken Curry
ingredients: chicken, onion, tomato, green chili, ginger, garlic, coconut, poppy seeds, sesame seeds, curry leaves, turmeric, red chili, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Dry roast coconut poppy seeds and sesame seeds; Grind to paste; Saute onion garlic ginger in oil until golden; Add tomato curry leaves and spices; Cook until oil separates; Add chicken and sear; Add coconut paste and mix; Add water simmer covered 25 min; Finish on high heat to thicken; Fiery Andhra style

name: Pesarattu Upma
ingredients: green moong dal, rice, ginger, green chili, semolina, onion, carrot, peas, mustard seeds, curry leaves, oil, salt
steps: Make pesarattu batter by grinding soaked moong with rice and green chili; Prepare upma by cooking semolina with vegetables and tempering; Spread pesarattu batter thin on tawa; Place a spoon of upma in center; Fold and serve with ginger chutney and sambar; Classic Andhra breakfast combination served at Tirupati

name: Chana Madra
ingredients: chickpeas, yogurt, ghee, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf, cumin, coriander, turmeric, asafoetida, salt
steps: Pressure cook chickpeas until soft; Heat ghee and add whole spices; Add asafoetida and cumin; Add beaten yogurt and stir continuously on medium heat; Do not let yogurt split; Add turmeric and coriander; Add chickpeas and mix; Simmer on very low heat 15 min; Adjust consistency; Traditional Himachali Dham dish

name: Sidu
ingredients: whole wheat flour, yeast, ghee, walnut, poppy seeds, cardamom, salt
steps: Mix flour with yeast and warm water and knead; Rest covered 2 hours until doubled; Punch down and divide; Stuff portions with walnut poppy seed and cardamom filling; Shape into buns; Steam in a steamer for 20 min; Remove and apply generous ghee; Serve with ghee and dal; Traditional Himachali bread served at celebrations

name: Aloo ke Gutke
ingredients: potato, mustard seeds, cumin, green chili, turmeric, coriander, dried red chili, oil, salt, coriander leaves
steps: Boil potatoes and cut into cubes; Heat oil until smoking; Add mustard seeds cumin and dried red chili; Add potato cubes and toss on high heat; Add turmeric and green chili; Toss vigorously until potatoes get crispy coating; Season with salt and coriander; Serve with bhang ki chutney or curd; Pahari staple

name: Kafuli
ingredients: spinach, fenugreek leaves, rice flour, garlic, ginger, green chili, turmeric, cumin, ghee, salt
steps: Wash and chop spinach and methi together; Pressure cook with turmeric salt and green chili; Cool and blend smooth; Return to pan; Mix rice flour in a little water and add to thicken; Simmer stirring on low heat; Heat ghee and temper cumin and garlic; Pour into kafuli; Serve with rice or mandua roti; Traditional Garhwali winter green

name: Chainsoo Dal
ingredients: black urad dal, garlic, dried red chili, cumin, turmeric, asafoetida, ghee, salt, coriander
steps: Dry roast black urad dal until fragrant; Grind roasted dal to coarse powder; Boil water with turmeric and salt; Add dal powder stirring to avoid lumps; Simmer on low heat 20 min until thick; Heat ghee and fry garlic until golden; Add dried red chili and cumin; Add asafoetida and pour into dal; Serve with bhaat and ghee

name: Litti Chokha
ingredients: whole wheat flour, sattu, garlic, ginger, green chili, ajwain, kalonji, mustard oil, eggplant, potato, tomato, lemon, salt
steps: Mix sattu with garlic ginger green chili ajwain kalonji and mustard oil for filling; Knead dough stuff with sattu and shape into balls; Roast litti over coal or gas flame until charred; Apply ghee generously; Roast eggplant and potato over flame; Peel and mash with tomato onion green chili and mustard oil for chokha; Serve litti with chokha

name: Sattu Paratha
ingredients: whole wheat flour, sattu, onion, green chili, ginger, ajwain, coriander, mustard oil, lemon, salt
steps: Mix sattu with finely chopped onion green chili ginger ajwain coriander mustard oil and lemon; Season with salt; Knead soft dough; Stuff sattu mixture into dough balls; Roll into thick parathas; Cook on tawa with oil on medium heat; Cook both sides until golden; Serve with pickle and curd; Bihar protein-rich breakfast

name: Bisi Bele Bath
ingredients: rice, toor dal, mixed vegetables, tamarind, coconut, coriander seeds, dried red chili, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, ghee, mustard seeds, curry leaves, cashews, salt, jaggery
steps: Pressure cook rice and toor dal together; Dry roast and grind coconut coriander chili and spices to masala; Cook vegetables in tamarind water; Add dal rice and bisi bele bath masala; Add jaggery and salt; Simmer mixing everything; Heat ghee and fry cashews then temper mustard seeds and curry leaves; Pour over bath; Serve hot

name: Akki Roti
ingredients: rice flour, onion, carrot, green chili, ginger, coriander, curry leaves, dill, coconut, oil, salt
steps: Mix rice flour with finely chopped vegetables herbs coconut and salt; Add hot water gradually and knead into soft but sticky dough; Take banana leaf or wet cloth; Pat dough into thin round roti; Cook on hot tawa drizzling oil around edges; Press gently; Cook until bottom is set then flip; Crisp on both sides; Serve with chutney

name: Ennegayi
ingredients: small brinjal, peanuts, sesame seeds, coconut, coriander seeds, cumin, dried red chili, tamarind, onion, garlic, oil, salt
steps: Dry roast peanuts sesame coconut and whole spices; Grind with tamarind to masala paste; Slit brinjals keeping stalk intact; Stuff masala paste inside each brinjal; Heat oil and saute onion garlic; Add stuffed brinjals gently; Add remaining masala and water; Cover and cook 20 min; Serve with jolada roti; North Karnataka specialty

name: Mangalorean Fish Curry
ingredients: kingfish, coconut, dried red chili, coriander seeds, cumin, turmeric, tamarind, onion, garlic, tomato, coconut oil, salt
steps: Grind coconut dried red chili coriander cumin turmeric and tamarind to smooth paste; Saute onion and garlic in coconut oil; Add ground paste and cook 5 min; Add tomato and cook; Add water and bring to boil; Add fish pieces and simmer gently 12 min; Do not stir; Serve with neer dosa or rice; Coastal Karnataka classic

name: Eromba
ingredients: fermented fish, potato, boiled vegetables, dried red chili, garlic, mustard oil, salt
steps: Boil potato and any seasonal vegetables until soft; Roast dried red chili and garlic; Pound roasted chili garlic and fermented fish together in mortar; Mash boiled vegetables roughly; Mix pounded paste with mashed vegetables; Add mustard oil and salt; Mix well with hands; Serve with rice; Traditional Manipuri condiment dish

name: Bamboo Shoot Pork Curry
ingredients: pork, bamboo shoot, onion, garlic, ginger, dried red chili, turmeric, king chili, oil, salt
steps: Boil bamboo shoots until tender and drain to remove bitterness; Saute onion garlic ginger in oil; Add pork pieces and sear well; Add dried red chili and turmeric; Add bamboo shoots and mix; Add water and cover; Cook on medium heat 40 min until pork is tender; Add king chili for heat if desired; Northeast staple

name: Lucknowi Dum Biryani
ingredients: basmati rice, chicken, yogurt, fried onion, saffron, rose water, kewra, ghee, whole spices, mint, coriander, garam masala, salt
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt and mild spices; Parboil rice with whole spices until 70 percent; Cook chicken in white gravy with mild spices until done; Layer chicken gravy in pot; Add rice on top with generous saffron milk rose water and kewra; Top with fried onion and mint; Seal with dough and dum cook 30 min; Awadhi style is mild fragrant and refined

name: Kolkata Biryani
ingredients: basmati rice, mutton, potato, boiled egg, onion, yogurt, saffron, rose water, kewra, ghee, whole spices, meetha attar, salt
steps: Marinate mutton overnight; Parboil rice with whole spices; Fry potato halves golden and set aside; Brown onion to golden; Layer mutton with potatoes and boiled eggs; Add parboiled rice; Drizzle saffron milk rose water kewra and meetha attar; Top with fried onion; Seal and dum cook 45 min; Unique for having potato and egg inside

name: Malabar Biryani
ingredients: jeerakasala rice, chicken, coconut milk, onion, tomato, mint, coriander, ghee, whole spices, garam masala, lemon, fried onion
steps: Parboil short-grain jeerakasala rice with whole spices; Cook chicken in rich masala with coconut milk; Layer in heavy pot with fried onion mint and coriander; Add rice layer and ghee; Seal with lid and dum cook 25 min; The short-grain rice absorbs coconut milk beautifully; Serve with raita pickle and pappadam; Kerala celebration dish

name: Dal Makhani
ingredients: whole black urad dal, kidney beans, butter, cream, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, garam masala, kasuri methi, oil, salt
steps: Soak urad dal and kidney beans overnight; Pressure cook until completely soft; In separate pan saute garlic ginger in butter; Add tomato puree and cook until thick; Add cooked dal and beans and mash slightly; Simmer on very low heat 30 min stirring occasionally; Add cream and kasuri methi; Slow cooking is key; Serve with naan

name: Butter Chicken
ingredients: chicken, yogurt, butter, cream, tomato, onion, garlic, ginger, kashmiri red chili, garam masala, kasuri methi, cardamom, sugar, salt
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt spices and lemon; Grill or bake until charred; Make makhani gravy by cooking onion garlic ginger and tomato; Blend smooth and strain; Return to pan with butter and kashmiri chili; Simmer until rich; Add grilled chicken and cream; Add kasuri methi and sugar; Simmer gently; The silky gravy is the soul

name: Tandoori Chicken
ingredients: chicken, yogurt, kashmiri red chili, garlic, ginger, lemon, cumin, coriander, garam masala, oil, salt, chaat masala
steps: Make deep cuts in chicken pieces; Mix yogurt with all spices garlic ginger and lemon for marinade; Coat chicken and marinate minimum 4 hours; Grill in very hot oven or over charcoal until charred on edges; Baste with oil halfway; Cook until juices run clear; Sprinkle chaat masala and lemon; Serve with onion rings and green chutney

name: Achari Chicken
ingredients: chicken, pickling spices, kalonji, fennel, mustard seeds, fenugreek, dried red chili, yogurt, onion, tomato, oil, salt
steps: Heat oil and fry all pickling spices including kalonji fennel mustard fenugreek and dried red chili; Add onion and brown; Add chicken and sear; Add tomato and cook until thick; Add yogurt and stir; Simmer covered 20 min; The pickling spices give a distinct tangy robust flavor different from regular curries; Serve with paratha

name: Appam with Vegetable Stew
ingredients: idli rice, coconut, yeast, potato, carrot, beans, peas, coconut milk, green chili, ginger, curry leaves, coconut oil, salt
steps: Soak and grind rice with coconut; Add yeast and ferment overnight; Make stew by cooking vegetables in thin coconut milk with green chili and ginger; Add thick coconut milk and curry leaves and simmer; Heat appam pan and pour batter; Swirl pan to coat edges thin; Cover and cook until center is set and edges are lacy; Serve appam with stew

name: Pongal
ingredients: raw rice, moong dal, black pepper, cumin, ginger, curry leaves, cashews, ghee, salt, asafoetida
steps: Dry roast moong dal until fragrant; Pressure cook rice and dal together until very soft and mushy; Heat ghee and fry cashews golden; Temper with black pepper cumin and curry leaves; Add ginger and asafoetida; Mix tempering into cooked rice and dal; Add more ghee and salt; Mash to porridge consistency; Serve with sambar and chutney

name: Rasam Vada
ingredients: urad dal, toor dal, tomato, tamarind, pepper, cumin, dried red chili, mustard seeds, curry leaves, asafoetida, ghee, coriander, salt
steps: Make medu vada batter and fry round vadas; Prepare rasam by boiling tomato and tamarind; Add pepper cumin powder and salt; Temper mustard seeds dried red chili and curry leaves in ghee; Add to rasam with coriander; Soak freshly fried vadas in hot rasam for 5 min; Serve warm; Vadas absorb rasam and become spongy and flavourful

name: Shahi Paneer
ingredients: paneer, onion, cashews, cream, yogurt, cardamom, cloves, mace, rose water, saffron, ghee, white pepper, salt
steps: Boil onion and cashews and blend to white paste; Heat ghee and cook paste with whole white spices; Add beaten yogurt stirring constantly; Add saffron milk and mace; Add paneer cubes and cream; Simmer gently 10 min; Add rose water and white pepper; The gravy should be ivory colored and rich; Serve with naan; Mughal heritage dish

name: Nihari
ingredients: lamb shank, onion, ginger, garlic, nihari masala, whole wheat flour, ghee, dried ginger, cardamom, fennel, bone marrow, salt, coriander
steps: Slow cook lamb shank with bone marrow and whole spices overnight or 4 hours; Mix wheat flour with water and add to thicken gravy; Add nihari masala and simmer; Add fried onion and ghee; Rest before serving; Serve topped with fresh ginger julienne coriander and fried onion; A Delhi breakfast eaten with naan; Slow cooking melts bone collagen

name: Dahi Gosht
ingredients: mutton, yogurt, onion, garlic, ginger, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf, ghee, white pepper, salt, coriander
steps: Marinate mutton in thick yogurt and whole spices for 2 hours; Brown onion in ghee until golden; Add mutton with marinade; Cook on high heat first; Lower heat and simmer covered; No water added — yogurt creates its own gravy; Cook until oil separates and mutton is tender; Garnish with coriander; Yogurt-braised mutton from Rajasthan

name: Undhiyu
ingredients: purple yam, raw banana, surti papdi, sweet potato, brinjal, fenugreek dumplings, coconut, garlic, green chili, coriander, sesame, oil, salt
steps: Make methi dumplings with besan and fenugreek leaves; Grind coconut garlic green chili and spices to masala paste; Layer all vegetables in a pot starting with harder ones; Add masala paste between layers; Drizzle oil and cover tightly; Cook on very low heat 40 min without opening; Shake pot to mix; Serve with puri; Gujarati winter harvest dish

name: Kofta Curry
ingredients: lauki, besan, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, cashews, cream, coriander, cumin, garam masala, oil, salt
steps: Grate lauki squeeze out water and mix with besan spices and coriander; Shape into balls and deep fry golden; Make rich gravy by blending boiled onion tomato and cashews; Cook gravy with spices until thick; Add cream and simmer; Place koftas in gravy just before serving; Pour hot gravy over koftas at table so they stay intact

name: Tindora Masala
ingredients: tindora, onion, garlic, ginger, tomato, cumin, coriander, turmeric, red chili, garam masala, oil, salt
steps: Wash and slit tindora lengthwise; Heat oil and saute garlic onion and ginger; Add tomato and spices and cook until thick; Add tindora and toss well; Cover and cook on medium heat 15 min stirring occasionally; Uncover and cook until tindora is tender and coated in masala; Finish with garam masala; Serve with roti

name: Ker Sangri
ingredients: ker berries, sangri beans, dried red chili, mustard seeds, cumin, turmeric, mango powder, coriander, oil, salt
steps: Soak ker berries and sangri overnight; Boil until tender and drain; Heat oil and temper mustard seeds cumin and dried red chili; Add soaked and boiled ker sangri; Toss well with turmeric and coriander; Add mango powder for tartness; Cook on low heat 10 min; Adjust seasoning; Traditional Rajasthani desert vegetable dish eaten with bajra roti

name: Dabeli
ingredients: potato, dabeli masala, tamarind chutney, garlic chutney, pomegranate, peanuts, sev, coriander, pav, butter
steps: Boil mash potato with dabeli masala and tamarind; Lightly toast pav with butter; Spread garlic chutney and tamarind chutney inside pav; Fill with potato mixture; Top with pomegranate seeds roasted peanuts sev and coriander; Close pav and press gently; Serve immediately; Kutchi street snack now popular across Gujarat and Mumbai

name: Ram Ladoo
ingredients: moong dal, chana dal, ginger, green chili, asafoetida, oil, radish, green chutney, tamarind chutney, chaat masala, salt
steps: Soak dals and grind coarsely with ginger green chili and asafoetida; Season with salt; Beat batter well for fluffiness; Deep fry small balls until golden crispy; Grate fresh radish and squeeze out water; Place ladoos in bowl; Top with grated radish; Drizzle both chutneys; Sprinkle chaat masala; Delhi winter street food staple

name: Frankie
ingredients: whole wheat flour, chicken or paneer, egg, onion, capsicum, tomato, green chutney, schezwan sauce, chaat masala, oil, salt
steps: Make soft roti dough and roll thin; Dip roti in beaten egg and cook on tawa with egg coating; Cook spiced chicken or paneer tikka filling separately; Place filling on roti; Add raw onion green chutney and schezwan sauce; Sprinkle chaat masala; Roll tightly in foil paper; Serve wrapped; Mumbai Tibbs Frankie inspired street roll

name: Pav Bhaji Fondue
ingredients: mixed vegetables, butter, pav bhaji masala, cheese, cream, garlic, onion, tomato, pav, coriander, lemon
steps: Make regular pav bhaji with generous butter; Add cream and grated cheese while bhaji is hot; Stir until cheese melts creating fondue texture; Pour into cast iron or serving pot; Top with more butter cheese and chopped coriander; Serve with toasted butter pav and raw onion; Cheesy twist on Mumbai classic for parties and cafes
